channel.title: Chart Breakers & Star Stories
channel.itunes:author: Family TVR
channel.itunes:summary: Did your favorite artist just break a record or a heart? The charts are screaming, and we have the inside story you won't hear anywhere else.
channel.description: The latest music trends, chart updates, and star stories from Billboard and beyond. Stay updated with the Pop Culture.

item.title: D4vd No Longer Signed to Sony Music Publishing or The·Team Amid Murder Arrest
item.description:
[Intro music plays]
Host: Welcome to our podcast, where we bring you the latest news and updates. Today, we're discussing a shocking development in the music industry. Singer-songwriter D4vd, known for his viral hit "Romantic Homicide", has been arrested on suspicion of murder.
[Soft music plays in the background]
Host: According to Billboard, D4vd was arrested by the Los Angeles Police Department on Thursday, April 16th, in connection with the death of 13-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez. Her dismembered body was found in D4vd's car last fall.
[Pause for a moment]
Host: D4vd was previously represented by Sony Music Publishing and The·Team for touring, but it's been learned that he's no longer part of their rosters. It's unclear when exactly he was dropped. His labels, Interscope and Darkroom Records, have not commented on whether he's still signed, but he's been removed from their websites.
[Soft music continues]
Host: The case against D4vd is still ongoing, and he's currently being held without bail. His attorneys have released a statement saying that the evidence will show D4vd did not murder Celeste Rivas Hernandez and was not the cause of her death.
[Pause again]
Host: D4vd's career took off in 2022 with his viral hit "Romantic Homicide", and he went on to release two EPs and a debut album, Withered. However, his tour was cut short and a deluxe version of Withered was shelved after Rivas' remains were found in his car.
[Outro music starts playing]
Host: We'll continue to follow this developing story and bring you updates as more information becomes available. Thank you for tuning in to our podcast. We'll be back with more news and updates soon.
[Outro music continues to play until the end]
